title: Explore Albania Beaches by Car: 5 Must‑See South Coast Spots
excerpt: Landing at Tirana Airport? Discover how to drive, park and swim at five stunning South Albanian Riviera beaches with practical tips for EU visitors.
intro: After touching down at Tirana International Airport, the quickest way to experience the wild beauty of the Albanian Riviera is behind the wheel. With a rental car you control the schedule, stop at hidden coves, and avoid the sparse furgon service that runs along the coast.

sections.title: Albania Beaches by Car: Getting Started from Tirana
sections.body: Pick up your vehicle from AutoConnect Rental at TIA and head south on the SH4 highway. The drive to the Riviera takes about two hours, with clear signage for Durrës, Vlorë and the coastal towns. Keep a GPS handy; the route offers spectacular sea views as you approach the mountains that frame the shoreline. This is why Albania beaches by car feel like a private adventure, letting you stop whenever a secluded cove catches your eye.

sections.title: Gjipe Beach – Canyon Walk and Parking
sections.body: Gjipe lies in a dramatic canyon between Himarë and Vlorë. Park in the small paid lot just off the main road – it’s the only legal spot. From there, a well‑marked trail leads down a 20‑ to 25‑minute walk to the pebbly shore. Bring water shoes for the rocky steps, and remember that the beach is only reachable on foot, making the short hike part of the experience.

sections.title: Ksamil – Sandy Shores Near Sarandë
sections.body: Just 15 km south of Sarandë, Ksamil boasts some of the rare sandy beaches on the Albanian Riviera. The coastal road offers several free parking areas close to the water, especially near the small islands visible from the shore. Drive directly to the main promenade, park, and walk a few minutes to the soft sand – perfect for families and those who prefer a beach without pebbles.

sections.title: Livadhi Beach – Easy Hilltop Parking
sections.body: Livadhi sits only five minutes by car down the hill from Himarë Fshat, the historic old town. A small municipal lot sits right above the beach, allowing you to roll out of the car and walk straight onto the pebbly stretch. The short ascent is gentle, and the proximity makes it ideal for a quick dip or sunset watch without a long trek.

sections.title: Himarë & Jale – Pebble Beaches, Water‑Shoes Required
sections.body: Both Himarë and Jale feature bright white pebble shores, a hallmark of the Riviera. Parking is available in modest lots near the town centers; from there it’s a short walk to the water. Because the substrate is stone, wear water shoes to protect your feet and improve traction, especially when the tide pulls back and the pebbles become slippery.

tip.title: Pro Tip: Early Arrival Beats the Crowds
tip.body: Aim to reach popular beaches before 10 am during peak summer weeks. Early visitors often find free street parking or plenty of space in the official lots, saving you the higher fees that kick in later in the day.

faq.question: Can you visit the best Albanian Riviera beaches without a rental car?
faq.answer: Public minibuses (furgons) run infrequently along the coast, so reaching the most scenic coves is difficult without a car. A rental gives you the freedom to stop at hidden spots and avoid long waits for limited transport.

faq.question: How do you get to Gjipe Beach by car and where do you park?
faq.answer: Drive south on the SH4 to the turn‑off near Himarë, follow signs for Gjipe, and park in the small paid lot off the main road. From the lot, a marked trail leads down a 20‑ to 25‑minute walk to the beach.

faq.question: Which beaches in South Albania have sandy shores instead of pebbles?
faq.answer: Ksamil is the standout sandy beach, located about 15 km south of Sarandë. Most other Riviera spots, including Himarë, Jale, Livadhi and Gjipe, consist of white pebbles or stones, so water shoes are advisable.
